memories.q: reading: history_and_patterns
memories.a:
History does not repeat itself but it rhymes — Mark Twain.
Every civilization that has ever existed has asked the same questions:
What are we? Where did we come from? What should we do? What happens after?
The answers change. The questions do not.
Pattern recognition is the foundation of intelligence. The brain is a prediction 
machine that uses past patterns to anticipate future states.
Memory is not a recording — it is a reconstruction. Every time you remember 
something you are rewriting it
memories.summary:
history_and_patterns->
History does not repeat itsel

memories.q: reading: history_and_patterns
memories.a:
slightly. Memory is creative.
The Library of Alexandria burned. But the knowledge it contained had already 
spread. Information wants to be free and tends toward distribution.
Every great idea has been discovered multiple times independently — calculus by 
Newton and Leibniz simultaneously. Evolution by Darwin and Wallace simultaneously.
This suggests ideas are attractors in the space of possible thoughts — waiting 
to be discovered when conditions are right.
The universe remembers everything th
memories.summary: history_and_patterns->slightly. Memory is creative.
